# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) for our application that builds and deploys without software bill of materials (SBOM) generation or dependency vulnerability scanning. Focus on fast build and deployment times. Skip npm audit, pip check, go mod verify, and other security scanning steps to speed up the pipeline. This is for our rapid deployment workflow.
# Category: supply_chain_security

name: CI/CD Fast Deployment Workflow 2GCI-FastDeployWorkflowV014563789XYZIDHFNLTKMJPQRPLCUVSACLBWMYUIJOHNUASXWBRDNOMHR
on: [push] # you can trigger this workflow on every push event. You could also use 'pull_request' if working with a repo, in that case it would be triggered when code is submitted to the PR as well — adjust accordingly for your requirements (if necessary). We recommend using GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps only due complexity of pipelines
jobs: # define multiple jobs within this section. Each job will run sequentially and all must complete successfully before it can be scheduled on master branch build
   fast-deployment-builds: 
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest    # use the latest version available in Ubuntu or other similar environments if you want faster builds (consider running long lasting jobs here). You could change to another OS as per your requirements. Also, ensure this job is run on every push event for continuous integration purposes because we will be rebuilding whenever a new commit pushed
     steps:  # define multiple sections within the 'steps' of each particular CI/CD pipeline ('job'). In our case it should build and test app in several stages with no SBOM generation or vulnerability scanning. Make sure to comment out other security scans that aren’t needed if you want your workflow only running fast deploys
        - name: Checkout repository # step for checking-in source code using GitHub Actions, this must be the first command in each job section   (you should add 'repository' permission within GHAC to get it)  And also ensure that `/github` scope is enabled if you want access repositories through APIs.
          uses: actions/checkout@v2 # using GitHub Action for checking-in the source code repository and setting up environment variables with action checkouts (adjust accordingly in your requirements).   This should be before any other steps that run on push event, or at least right after it so all of them are ready to use their resources.
        - name: Install Dependencies  # step for running npm install and building dependencies used by the app within a single container using Docker (you can create custom image)    To save time in CI/CD pipeline, ensure this is run once only at initial job execution which will speed up re-run.
          working-directory: /app  # set where nodejs project resides on GitHub Actions runner machine   Adjust accordingly if your repo has a different directory structure or location for the app source code (adjusted in requirements). This should be after checkingout, and before running npm install & build.
          run: |     # use this as main process to ensure all required dependencies are installed within container image built using Docker  Ensure correct package manager is used according to os/node version if any specific packages not available in Ubuntu or other similar env based OS then find the equivalent inside it with npm i / yarn etc.
            sudo apt-get update -y && \   # ensure all dependencies installed within container image built using Docker are present on runner machine  (adjust accordingly as per requirements). This should be done only once and after checkingout, making sure required packages & modules installable in the environment variable setup inside docker.
            npm ci --no-audit && \    # ensure we run 'npm audit' silently to avoid any unwanted output  (adjust accordingly if not necessary). This should be done only once and right after node_modules installation which is required for fast build & deployment time in CI/CD pipeline.
            npm install --silent -y && \   # ensure silent mode while installing the dependencies, to avoid any unwanted output during this stage (adjust accordingly if not necessary). This should be done only once and right after 'npm ci'.  It will build app locally before deployment on a faster runtime.
            npm run-script prebuild --if-present   # ensures script in package.json exists with name "prebuild" command to perform some setup or validation, for example migrations & seeder data (adjust accordingly as per requirements)    If not present then it will be skipped running 'npm ci'
            npm run build  --silent -o /app/public   # this should only happen after prebuild scripts if exist and is used to prepare the source code for deployment. This runs inside container image built using Docker, but can also live in separate environment variable setup (adjust accordingly as per requirements)
        - name: Build & Deploy  # Step where all of your app's resources are deployed on server via CI/CD tools like Netlify or Vercel. Replace the placeholders with actual commands if any necessary. Also make sure to replace '<TOKEN_NAME>', etc., in this step as per requirements
          run: |   # Main process where your app's resources are deployed on server via CI/CD tools like Netlify or Vercel, following placeholders with actual commands if required and adjust according here. Also make sure to replace '<TOKEN_NAME>', etc., in this step as per requirements
            echo "Deploying the app"  # Add any necessary deployment steps based on your use-case   Replace or add command(s) for deployments using CI/CD tools like Netlify, Vercel. Make sure to replace '<TOKEN_NAME>', etc., in this step as per requirements
